food_subgroup: Fried chicken combo meal — boneless wings, classic bone-in wings, chicken tenders, french fries, and ranch dipping sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-component fried chicken family combo meal. FOODON_00002490 (fried chicken food product) is used as the closest defensible anchor. The full meal includes wings, tenders, fries, and dipping sauce.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, fried — used as the primary anchor for the fried chicken components of this combo meal
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers fried chicken meat. No composite family-pack code exists in FoodEx2. The fries and ranch dip components are not separately coded here; the anchor reflects the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept maps to a multi-component fried chicken family combo meal at this level of specificity.

serving.description: 1 family pack (4–5 persons): 12 boneless wings, 12 classic wings, 6 tenders, 4 regular fries, 4 ranch dips
serving.natural_language_portion: One family pack serves 4 to 5 people and contains 12 boneless wings in two flavors, 12 classic bone-in wings in two flavors, 6 chicken tenders in one flavor, 4 portions of regular french fries, and 4 ranch dipping sauces, with an estimated total weight of approximately 2,200 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken wing, fried, coated; Chicken breast strips, breaded and fried; French fries, frozen, oven-heated; Ranch dressing; composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry covers this exact multi-component family combo meal. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated as a weighted composite of four USDA FoodData Central entries: (1) Chicken wing, fried, coated (FDC ID 171477) — representing classic bone-in wings; (2) Chicken breast strips, breaded and fried (FDC ID 173622) — representing boneless wings and tenders; (3) French fries, frozen, oven-heated (FDC ID 170720) — representing the fries component; (4) Salad dressing, ranch, regular (FDC ID 172459) — representing ranch dip. Estimated component weight proportions: fried chicken components ~60%, fries ~30%, ranch dip ~10%. All values are per 100 g of the composite edible portion.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; fried chicken and french fries analogous entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists for this exact Wingstop family combo meal. GI is estimated as a weighted composite: breaded fried chicken GI ~60 (University of Sydney GI Database, analogous entry for fried battered chicken); french fries GI ~75 (University of Sydney GI Database, french fries entry); ranch dip GI ~30 (low-carbohydrate condiment, estimated). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution: fries contribute ~55% of total carbohydrate, fried chicken breading ~40%, ranch dip ~5%. Composite GI ≈ 65, classified as medium. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 451 g carbohydrate in full pack) / 100 ≈ 29.5 per person portion (full pack divided by 4.5 persons average). Estimation basis documented per Project Veritas v2 GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; estimated ~2,640 mg per person serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single person's share of the family pack provides an estimated 2,640 mg of sodium — exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg — primarily from seasoned breading, ranch dip, and frying oil.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load from breaded coating and french fries, with a medium GI of 65 and high glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or consume only a very small portion, as the combination of breaded fried chicken and french fries delivers a high glycemic load (~29.5 GL per person share) that is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium from deep-frying and ranch dip
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal, as the deep-fried chicken and ranch dip contribute substantial saturated fat (~3.9 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), cholesterol (~62 mg/100 g), and sodium (~480 mg/100 g), all of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Wingstop Family Pack (4–5 pax) is a large-format fried chicken combo meal sold at Wingstop restaurants in Singapore, including outlets at City Square Mall and Funan. The pack contains 12 boneless wings in two flavors, 12 classic bone-in wings in two flavors, 6 chicken tenders in one flavor, 4 portions of regular french fries, and 4 ranch dipping sauces, designed to serve 4 to 5 people. The chicken pieces are coated in seasoned wheat flour breading and deep-fried in vegetable oil, then tossed in Wingstop's signature dry-rub or sauced flavors such as Original Hot, Lemon Pepper, Garlic Parmesan, or Louisiana Rub. The boneless wings are made from breast meat, while the classic wings are bone-in with a crispy exterior and juicy interior. The tenders are elongated strips of breast meat with a lighter breading. The french fries are thin-cut and salted, and the ranch dip is a creamy, herb-based condiment. Nutritionally, this meal is energy-dense at approximately 285 kcal per 100 g, with significant protein (~14.5 g/100 g), high fat (~16.8 g/100 g including saturated and trans fats), and moderate carbohydrates (~20.5 g/100 g) from the breading and fries. Sodium content is high (~480 mg/100 g), making this meal a concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The meal is certified Halal and is a popular family dining option in Singapore's fast-food fried chicken segment. It is priced at SGD 64.50 and represents a value-for-money sharing format. The GI is estimated at 65 (medium), driven primarily by the french fries component.

confidence_reason: No single approved source covers this exact multi-component Wingstop family combo meal. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite of four USDA FoodData Central entries for fried chicken, breaded chicken strips, french fries, and ranch dressing. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried battered chicken and french fries. Confidence is medium because the component proportions and exact Wingstop recipes are not publicly disclosed in an approved nutritional database.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the estimated composite edible portion of the full family pack. The full pack weighs approximately 2,200 g edible. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimation. 3. Trans fat is estimated at 0.2 g/100 g based on USDA data for commercially fried chicken; actual value may vary by Wingstop frying oil specification. 4. The GI of 65 is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. 5. Halal status is preserved from the input cuisine tags, consistent with Wingstop Singapore's Halal certification. 6. Price is SGD 64.50 as reported by field survey data for both Wingstop City Square Mall and Wingstop Funan outlets. 7. Serving size of 2,200 g is an estimate based on typical weights: 12 boneless wings (~480 g), 12 classic wings (~600 g), 6 tenders (~300 g), 4 regular fries (~600 g), 4 ranch dips (~220 g). 8. Glycemic load per serving is calculated per person (full pack ÷ 4.5 average persons): carbohydrate per person ≈ 45.4 g; GL = (65 × 45.4) / 100 ≈ 29.5. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite entries and are approximate. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entries used.
